[Overview]
Emeritus Professor Hans Lambers has made available funds to establish the Hi Vallee Research Award. This research award is provided to enable research students at the University of Western Australia to undertake research directly relevant to Hi Vallee, including but not limited to fieldwork and gathering research data.

[Eligibility]
To be eligible to apply for a scholarship, an applicant must be—
a) enrolled as a full-time student in an Honours, Masters or HDR at UWA or be a visiting PhD student by the date of the commencement of the award; and
b) undertaking research in the natural sciences related to Hi Vallee.

Applications are called for by the School of Biological Sciences and must be emailed to the School Office (schoolops-sbs@uwa.edu.au) by the advertised closing date.
Applications must include the following documents:
a) a statement of no more than 500 words addressing the eligibility criteria and selection criteria set out in Clauses 7 and 9;
b) a current Curriculum Vitae; and
c) a budget for the proposed research expenditure.

[Benefits]
$2,000 to enable research students to undertake research directly relevant to Hi Vallee.

Title: UWA Winthrop Scholarship
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: UWA Winthrop Scholarship
INFO: STATUS: Open | APPLICATIONS OPEN: 05/01/2026 | APPLICATIONS CLOSE: 20/06/2026 | VALUE: $80,000.00 | NUMBER OFFERED: 10

[Overview]
The UWA Winthrop Scholarship is one of our University's Prestigious Scholarships. This scholarship program has been developed to provide high school leavers with an opportunity to undertake an undergraduate degree course and then a postgraduate coursework degree at the University. The scholarship is for graduating high-achieving, high school students. They will show significant academic excellence, are community minded and can demonstrate achievements in a co-curricular activity throughout Year 11 and 12. A statement evidencing their leadership ambitions will also be required.

[Eligibility]
Please note, the current application period for this scholarship is for current year 12 High School students planning on commencing their undergraduate degree at UWA in Semester 1, 2027.

To be eligible to be considered for the scholarship, an applicant must—
(a) be a domestic student;
(b) in the year prior to commencing an undergraduate course at the University—
(i) have accepted an Early Offer to the University, or applied through the Tertiary Institutions Service Centre (TISC) whilst in Year 12 for a place at the University, nominating a course at the University as their first preference in the first round of offers;
(ii) achieve an ATAR of 95 or above
(c) be a commencing student;
(d) be enrolled, or eligible to enrol—
(i) full-time and
(ii) in an undergraduate degree course.

[Benefits]
The UWA Winthrop Scholarship is one of our University's Prestigious Scholarships. Students who show significant academic potential, community-minded behaviours and outstanding achievements throughout Year 11 and 12 in co-curricular activities with an ambition to develop their leadership ability, are encouraged to apply.

Recipients of the scholarship will be invited, and expected, to participate actively in a university prescribed leadership development and mentorship program. This includes membership of the University’s mentor scheme, participation in other selected activities and periodic visits to schools to promote university education in general.

Value: up to $80,000*

$10,000 per annum (six semesters undergraduate plus one year honours if applicable) and a further
$10,000 per annum (maximum eight semesters postgraduate)

(Conditions apply including maintaining a minimum WAM)

Title: Ciara Glennon Memorial Law Scholarship
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: Ciara Glennon Memorial Law Scholarship
INFO: STATUS: Open | APPLICATIONS OPEN: 04/03/2026 | APPLICATIONS CLOSE: 25/03/2026 | VALUE: $10,000.00 | NUMBER OFFERED: 1

[Overview]
Ashurst, a leading global law firm, in conjunction with the Glennon Family, offers this scholarship in memory of the late Ciara Eilish Glennon, a graduate of The University of Western Australia and alumna of the firm, to assist a deserving student experiencing financial hardship to continue and complete their studies towards a Juris Doctor at the University.

[Eligibility]
Applications should be submitted via the online form. Click on Apply Now to access this form. We cannot accept hard copies or emailed applications.

Applications must include—
(a) supporting documentation establishing satisfaction of the eligibility criteria;
(b) a statement by the applicant addressing the selection criteria; and
(c) three written references from referees who collectively are able to address, from personal knowledge of the candidate, the selection criteria.

To be eligible to apply for the scholarship, an applicant must—
(a) be an Australian citizen or permanent resident;
(b) have completed at least one year of full-time study towards the Juris Doctor degree;
(c) be enrolled, or eligible to enrol—
(i) full-time unless the selection body is satisfied that there are exceptional circumstances which preclude full-time enrolment; and
(ii) in a Juris Doctor degree course; and
(d) have completed all eight first year units.

[Benefits]
Ashurst, a leading global law firm, in conjunction with the Glennon Family, offers this scholarship in memory of the late Ciara Eilish Glennon, a graduate of The University of Western Australia and alumna of the firm, to assist a deserving student experiencing financial hardship to continue and complete their studies towards a Juris Doctor at the University.

The recipient of the scholarship will receive $10,000 as well as a paid, two week clerkship with Ashurst and a paid, two week internship with Crown Resorts Ltd.

Title: Generowicz Scholarship
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: Generowicz Scholarship
INFO: STATUS: Closed | VALUE: $24,000.00

[Overview]
Mr Mirek Generowicz, an engineer of 30 years' standing, donates funds annually to provide a scholarship to enable an Indigenous student or a student from an educationally disadvantaged group to undertake an undergraduate degree course with a Bachelor of Science major or a major applicable to law at The University of Western Australia ('the University'). Preference is given to Indigenous Australians and holders of an Australian Permanent Humanitarian Visa; however other applicants experiencing educational disadvantage are also encouraged to apply.

[Eligibility]
To be eligible to apply for a scholarship an applicant must be—
(a) enrolled or eligible to enrol in an undergraduate degree course with a Bachelor of Science major, Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) or major applicable to law1; and either
(b) an Indigenous Australian;2 or
(c) a holder of an Australian Permanent Humanitarian Visa; or
(d) experiencing educational disadvantage which is demonstrated by being one of the following:
(i) a recent school leaver (under 20 years of age) from a low Socioeconomic Status school;
(ii) a student from a regional or remote background;3
(iii) a student with a disability or chronic medical problem;
(iv) a student with carer responsibilities.

1 See the Law School for more information.

2 An Australian Indigenous person is a person who:
• is of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent;
• identifies as an Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; and
• is accepted as an Australian Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander in the community in which he or she lives or has lived.

3 To be considered for a scholarship based on rural or regional status, students need to prove that they have lived in a rural or remote area. See https://www.scholarships.uwa.edu.au/students/undergrad/rural

[Benefits]
To enable an Indigenous student or a student from an educationally disadvantaged group to undertake an undergraduate degree course with a Bachelor of Science major, Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) or a major applicable to law at The University of Western Australia (‘the University’).

Preference is given to Indigenous Australians and holders of an Australian Permanent Humanitarian Visa; however other applicants experiencing educational disadvantage are also encouraged to apply.

The objective of the scholarship is to support the next generation of leaders studying at The University of Western Australia, by helping promising young Australians achieve their potential for the benefit of future generations of Australians.

[Eligibility]

A Round Table Conference will be held at Admiralty House in Sydney with all Awardees, with press coverage organized. This will be held in late September, 2025.

Awardees are presented with their Certificate by the Governor-General or her representative at a gala Awards Ceremony associated with the Order of Australia Association’s Annual Conference Dinner. Title: INDRAN CHELVA MEMORIAL SCHOLARSHIP
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: INDRAN CHELVA MEMORIAL SCHOLARSHIP
INFO: STATUS: Closed | VALUE: $3,000.00

[Overview]
Friends of Dr Indran Chelvanayagam have contributed $60,000 to establish a fund for the purpose of providing, in perpetuity, an annual travel scholarship in rural ophthalmology for students enrolled in the course for the degree of Doctor of Medicine (MD) at The University of Western Australia ('the University'). This scholarship is dedicated to the memory of Dr Indran Chelva who committed his career to working in regional and rural practise. In particular, he regularly visited the Kimberley to provide services in Indigenous communities and always enthused about these visits. His life was tragically cut short but he is remembered fondly and respectfully by his colleagues.

[Eligibility]
To be eligible to apply for a scholarship an applicant must—

(a) be enrolled in the MD course at the University;

(b) be enrolled, or eligible to enrol, in either—

(i) IMED5311 Integrated Medical Practice 2 Part A and IMED5312 Integrated Medical practice 2 Part B; or
(ii) RMED5312 Integrated Rural Medical Practice 1 and RMED5321 Integrated Rural Medical Practice 2; and

(c) be able to demonstrate a commitment to a future career in rural and remote medicine by way of one of the following:

(i) being enrolled in the Rural service learning or research specialisation of the MD course with a project related to ophthalmological practice in regional or remote areas; or
(ii) be actively involved in rural and remote community service.

[Benefits]
The successful recipient will spend and agreed period during their year 3 studies or immediately prior to the commencement of their year 4 studies accompanying an ophthalmologist who is providing ophthalmic care to regional or remote communities in Western Australia, by contributing towards expenses associated with travel, including airfares, accommodation and ancillary costs.

Title: OAAF SCHOLARSHIP IN SCIENCE: IT
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: OAAF SCHOLARSHIP IN SCIENCE: IT
INFO: STATUS: Closed | VALUE: $45,000.00

[Overview]
The McCusker Charitable Foundation supports The Order of Australia Association Foundation (OAAF) to award a prestigious and generous scholarship to eligible second year students in a defined course of study in Science: Information Technology and demonstrate financial need. The objective of the Scholarship is to support the next generation of leaders studying at The University of Western Australia, by helping promising young Australians achieve their potential for the benefit of future generations of Australians.

[Eligibility]
To be eligible to be considered for this scholarship, a applicant must -

(a) be an Australian Citizen;
(b) be in their first Undergraduate degree;
(c) be in their second year of their undergraduate degree;
(d) have completed at least 48 credit points but no more than 90 credit points;
(e) have a WAM of 75 or above;
(f) demonstrated need for financial assistance;
(g) be enrolled full-time in one of the following degrees:
(i) Bachelor of Advanced Computer Science (Honours); or
(ii) Bachelor of Engineering (Honours); or
(iii) Bachelor of Science or Bachelor of Philosophy (Honours) majoring in Computer Science, Cybersecurity or Data Science

Applicants must apply via the online application form and you will need to provide evidence of financial circumstances which can include, a Centrelink Statement and/or payslips from the past two months and/or ATO Notice of Assessment for the 2023/2024 Financial Year.

Selection is based on high academic achievement, demonstrated qualities of leadership and community involvement, need for financial assistance and how you would benefit from a mentor.

Students who are shortlisted will be notified by the 26th of August 2025 and must be available for an interview on the 1st of September 2025.

[Benefits]
The Foundation, so far, has awarded 60 Scholarships to undergraduates in all States and Territories.

The Awardee must provide a commitment that in the years to come, and subject to their availability, they would be willing to engage with fellow Alumni, as well as the activities of the Foundation and the Order of Australia Association.

A Round Table Conference will be held at Admiralty House in Sydney with all Awardees, with press coverage organized. This will be held in late September, 2025.

Awardees are presented with their Certificate by the Governor-General or her representative at a gala Awards Ceremony associated with the Order of Australia Association’s Annual Conference Dinner. The Awardee’s immediate family, the Scholarship Donor, the Mentor, and a University representative are also invited to the Awards Ceremony. The Annual Conference will be held in Brisbane on 2 May, 2026.

More information about the scholarship can be found here - https://oaafoundation.org.au/

Please direct any questions regarding the scholarship to Scholarships via askUWA or by calling on 6488 3235 and choosing 6 as the option

Title: HARRY TAMBAKIS MEMORIAL SCHOLARSHIP
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: HARRY TAMBAKIS MEMORIAL SCHOLARSHIP
INFO: STATUS: Closed | VALUE: $5,000.00

[Overview]
Wallbridge Gilbert Aztec (WGA), in consultation with the Tambakis family, has donated funds to The University of Western Australia ('the University') to establish a scholarship, in memory of the late Harry Tambakis, to encourage and assist a high-achieving student studying Civil Engineering, to continue their studies at the University. Harry Tambakis was a Civil Engineering graduate of the University and a much-loved engineer at WGA.

[Eligibility]
To be eligible to apply for the scholarship, an applicant must—
(a) demonstrate—
(i) a genuine passion and personal interest in engineering and the role that it plays within our society;
(ii) creative problem solving and innovation; and

INFO: STATUS: Closed | VALUE: $15,000.00

[Overview]
The Centenary Trust for Women has provided funds to the University of Western Australia (`the University') to establish a scholarship in recognition of Margaret Herley's support of the Trust. This scholarship aims to assist and encourage an eligible female student, who demonstrates an interest in Humanities, to undertake a major in History or English. For the past two decades Margaret Herley has served on the Centenary Trust for Women, promoting the on-going studies by female students at the University. In more recent years, Margaret chaired the Scholarship Sub-Committee which oversaw the naming and the criteria set for many of the Centenary Trust scholarships now in existence. Her education commenced in Cunderdin, a small town in rural Western Australia, then secondary years spent in boarding school followed by years at The University of Western Australia concluding in a Bachelor of Arts, majoring in History. In the years following, Margaret taught in both WA and in NSW predominantly in the areas of History and English, completing her professional career as Principal of Iona Presentation College.

Title: THE RENU SHARMA SCHOLARSHIP IN ASTRONOMY AND ASTROPHYSICS
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: THE RENU SHARMA SCHOLARSHIP IN ASTRONOMY AND ASTROPHYSICS
INFO: STATUS: Closed | VALUE: $10,000.00

[Overview]
The University of Western Australia's (`the University') node of the International Centre for Radio Astronomy Research is funding a scholarship to assist and encourage a high-achieving female and/or indigenous student to undertake the Astronomy and Astrophysics specialisation of the Master of Physics. Dr. Renu Sharma has been the Chief Operating Officer of the International Centre for Radio Astronomy Research from its establishment in 2009 until 2025. She has been the driving force behind ICRAR?s activities in the diversity, equity and inclusion space, with particular emphasis on supporting and empowering women and other underrepresented groups in STEM.

[Eligibility]
To be eligible to apply for a scholarship, an applicant must—

(a) be an enrolled domestic student

(b) be a female or of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent;

(c) have applied for a place to commence a Master of Physics degree course with a specialisation in Astronomy and Astrophysics, or be enrolled in the Combined Bachelor of Science Frontier Physics and Master of Physics and have completed 96 points of undergraduate study

(d) have an ICRAR staff member as the primary supervisor of their research project component.

[Benefits]
Dr. Renu Sharma is the Chief Operating Officer and Director of Translation and Impact at the International Centre for Radio Astronomy Research (ICRAR), a joint venture between Curtin University and the University of Western Australia (UWA).

Dr. Sharma joined ICRAR in 2009 as a founding Executive team member and since then has been responsible for leading ICRAR’s management, reporting, and compliance requirements and building a supportive organisational culture. She has been committed to ensuring diversity, equity, and inclusion at ICRAR and has enabled creation of an inclusive environment that enables growth and supports the dignity of all. She successfully launched the 'ICRAR visiting fellowship for senior women in astronomy' that has attracted national and international female role model astronomers to ICRAR since 2015. Dr. Sharma's commitment has contributed to ICRAR’s recognition for its excellence in diversity, equity, and inclusion practices, winning the Bronze in 2015, Silver in 2017, and Gold in 2019, 2021 and 2023 at the Astronomical Society for Astronomy's Pleiades awards.

Dr. Sharma has worked with academic and administrative leadership across universities, State and Federal Government and with the Square Kilometre Array (SKA) project. She has held several senior management roles, including serving as a member of the Vice Chancellor's Equity and Diversity Committee, Equity and Diversity Advisor, and Chair of Culturally and Linguistically Diverse Working Group.

Outside of her work, Dr. Sharma is passionate about writing poetry in Hindi, she is a Trustee of Hindi Samaj of WA (2023-25), a radio presenter at the 95.3 EBA and has been recognised by Indian Society of WA for her personal excellence in 2017, by Hindu Council of Australia in 2023 by Gargi Award for her contributions. In 2024, she featured in a coffee table book featuring 50 inspiring Indo-Australian women who made a difference.

Title: Cancer Council of WA Student Vacation Research Scholarships
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: Cancer Council of WA Student Vacation Research Scholarships
INFO: STATUS: Closed | VALUE: $3,000.00

[Overview]
Cancer Council Western Australia aims to minimise the effect of cancer on our community through advocacy, research and education and by providing people affected by cancer with support to enhance their quality of life. Applications are being sought for one PhD Top Up Scholarship of up to $12,000pa for three years to be held in conjunction with a Research Training Program (RTP) Stipend or equivalent.

[Eligibility]
Eligibility requirements:
- Students enrolled full-time in the year when the application is received.
- Students who have completed two years of an appropriate course work degree and are enrolled in at least the third year of the course work degree in the disciplines relevant to cancer control or treatment (when the application is received). Alternatively, medical students currently at the end of their third year or post graduate medical students who have not already completed a research degree are eligible.
- Most of the research work (assessed as 75% or more) should be conducted in or through legitimate Western Australian Higher Education and/or Medical Research Institutions.

Applicants are ineligible if:
- They are enrolled in or have completed a research degree (e.g. Honours).
- They are enrolled part-time in the year when the application is received.
- They have not completed two years of an appropriate degree course when the application is received.
- They are previous recipients of a James Crofts Hope Foundation Student Vacation Research Scholarship, an Abbie Basson Sarcoma Foundation Student Vacation Research Scholarship or a Cancer Council WA Student Vacation Research Scholarship.
- They are current recipients of a full-time scholarship intended to provide an equivalent stipend/salary of an amount equal to or greater than $3,000 may be ineligible (assessed on a case by case basis).
- Students who are currently employed in the area (e.g. third or fourth year student working as a part time research assistant).

More information can be found here - https://cancerwa.asn.au/research/funding-opportunities/

[Benefits]
These Scholarships are intended to expose talented university students to cancer research. Applications are encouraged from any area of cancer research related to the causes, diagnosis, treatment and/or prevention of cancer.

More information and information on how to apply can be found here - https://cancerwa.asn.au/research/funding-opportunities/
